beats.narration_text: Talofa. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of hydrogen atom. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of hydrogen atom

beats.narration_text:
Hydrogen Atom is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. E_n = −13.6/n² eV  |  Selection rules: Δℓ = ±1, Δm = 0, ±1  | 
  λ = 1240/ΔE[eV] nm  |  Click two levels to inspect any transition. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet:
Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Hydrogen Atom. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript.  Verify: E_n = −13.6/n² eV  |  Selection rules: Δℓ = ±1, Δm = 0, ±1  | 
  λ = 1240/ΔE[eV] nm  |  Click two levels to inspect any . Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Hydrogen Atom. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript.  Verify: E_n = −13.6/n² eV  |  Selection rules: Δℓ = ±1, Δm = 0, ±1  |

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.
beats.new_visual_element: Onda code-block: core JS (integrator + slider handler)
beats.prompt: Show the core physics loop from qm2-hydrogen-transitions.html

beats.narration_text: At the default settings: hydrogen atom. Watch what the simulation shows.
Now drag main control. Watch the physics respond in real time.
beats.new_visual_element: Slate: narrate slider sweep of main control

beats.narration_text:
E_n = −13.6/n² eV  |  Selection rules: Δℓ = ±1, Δm = 0, ±1  | 
  λ = 1240/ΔE[eV] nm  |  Click two levels to inspect any transition. Exactly as the equations predict.
One prompt. A live demonstration of hydrogen atom. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Hydrogen Atom simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

props.command: Take this Hydrogen Atom simulation and add a second slider that controls the main parameter. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.

beats.narration_text: Hydrogen Atom — Energy Levels & Transitions. Medhavy.
